title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, from Roslindale is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is running with Team Eye and Ear to support Massachusetts Eye and Ear where she has been a patient.
Mass. Eye and Ear is renowned for research and houses the only 24-hour emergency ENT department in Massachusetts.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC's top NBA broadcast team, joining Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson in August 2025.
Legler is ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst, having joined in 2000, and will call the NBA Finals in June.
Celtics-Cavaliers game will be covered by Legler, with interest in Jaylen Brown's and Jayson Tatum's performances noted.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after a 298-day absence, posting 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Maine native Cooper Flagg debuted at TD Garden with 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Dallas Mavericks.
Flagg received a warm welcome despite the Celtics' 120-100 victory, drawing cheers from a crowd that included many Maine fans.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned from an Achilles tear, scoring 15 points and contributing 12 rebounds and 7 assists against the Mavericks.
Charles Barkley remains skeptical of the Celtics as Eastern Conference favorites, continuing to favor the Cavaliers despite Tatum's return.
The Celtics have tied for the most NBA wins since January 1, while Cleveland holds an 8-1 record with James Harden since February.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers of Pittsfield is accused of impersonating an Army veteran for over 30 years to gain benefits.
Sommers allegedly accessed thousands in Social Security benefits and $30,000 in VA care using a stolen identity.
Federal charges include making false statements; Sommers faces up to five years in prison and a $250,000 fine.

title: Watch: Body cam video shows officer rescuing man, 78, from burning truck
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Easton police officer Dean Soucie and two bystanders rescued a 78-year-old man from a burning pickup truck.
The incident followed a crash involving three vehicles on Turnpike Street; the driver sustained serious, non-life-threatening injuries.
The crash is under investigation; Turnpike Street was closed for hours as authorities processed the scene.

title: US attorney going after money forfeited by Mass. resident in romance crypto fraud scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Federal prosecutors seek to recover $327,000 in cryptocurrency linked to a romance scam targeting a Massachusetts resident.
The scam involved a fake online relationship where the victim was persuaded to invest in cryptocurrency through dating app contact.
The funds were laundered through several wallets and seized by authorities in August 2025, linked to alleged money laundering.

title: Jayson Tatum’s fashion also makes a return in his comeback from Achilles tear
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics with a double-double performance after recovering from an Achilles tear.
Tatum's pregame attire featured a mix of Parisian luxury, '90s nostalgia, and streetwear, catching widespread attention.
His outfit included high-end denim, a crisp collared shirt, and 'Bruce Lee' Jordan Tatum 4 Player Exclusive sneakers.

title: She survived a brain tumor. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tracy Haisley, a brain tumor survivor, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her recovery journey.
Haisley, from Columbia, SC, underwent surgery and extensive rehab, making running the marathon a significant achievement.
Haisley joins the Boston Public Library marathon team, advocating for public libraries' importance and continued support.

title: What Jaylen Brown said of Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed excitement for Jayson Tatum's return to the Celtics following his recovery from a ruptured Achilles.
Tatum contributed 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists in a 120-100 Celtics win over the Mavericks.
Brown emphasized the team's unity and readiness to tackle challenges ahead for a deep playoff run.
